run_context: Mode 1 cleanup: patching final 2 open issues (both minor, WIN-025). All 18 critical/major issues are in patched status awaiting human application.

The dome's claimed distinguishing feature
patches.replace:
), and by Chernogor & Holub themselves, who explicitly attribute the observed variations to suppression of ionospheric E-region conductivity and disruption of the Sq dynamo current system during the eclipse shadow — the same mechanism Chapman quantified in 1933. The Curto et al. (2006) multi-eclipse study confirmed ±5–30 nT surface perturbations arising consistently across different eclipses, solar cycle phases, and geographic tracks: standard physics predicts this response for any total solar eclipse, not as a one-time fit to 2024 data.

The dome's claimed distinguishing feature
patches.rationale: Addresses ISS-546 (add multi-eclipse comparison) and ISS-547 (strengthen Chernogor attribution) in one edit. ISS-546: adds sentence showing Curto et al. 2006 confirmed consistent eclipse response across multiple eclipses/solar cycles, closing off cherry-picking defense. ISS-547: upgrades 'who attribute the observed effects to ionospheric conductivity changes' to 'who explicitly attribute the observed variations to suppression of ionospheric E-region conductivity and disruption of the Sq dynamo current system during the eclipse shadow', making clear this is the paper's own mechanistic conclusion, not our external interpretation.
